@@ -0,0 +1,155 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a980
+project: spatial-nav
+title: Effective moniker is a path through the focus hierarchy — eliminate duplicate-moniker ambiguity that surfaces as cross-layer nav
+---
+## What
+
+Make the kernel's effective moniker a fully-qualified path through the focus hierarchy: `/window/inspector/field:T1.title` is a different moniker than `/window/board/column:todo/card:T1/field:title`. The leaf string consumers pass today (e.g. `field:T1.title`) becomes a **local segment**, not a global identifier. The kernel never again sees the same moniker registered twice.
+
+User direction:
+> "I reject that this has anything to do with claim on mount — because that just isn't happening. I'm needing to click fields to focus them, and navigation is still spilling out of the inspector to the board. You are crossing layers. I keep saying it. But that is a good discovery — so the actual effective moniker needs to be a fully qualified 'path' not just the leaf — when we focus a thing it is like /window/layer/zone.../scope instead of pretending the identifier of the scope is unique."
+
+## Concrete bug confirmed in the running app's log
+
+After opening an inspector on `task:01KQAWVDS931PADB0559F2TVCS`, the macOS unified log emits:
+
+```
+duplicate moniker registered against two distinct keys —
+spatial_focus_by_moniker will resolve non-deterministically
+moniker=field:task:01KQAWVDS931PADB0559F2TVCS.title
+op="find_by_moniker"
+first_key=4ab0e988-9d12-48c6-94f3-2bfb783a7fdb
+second_key=0fac21b6-2849-4661-ae49-222bf08149e2
+```
+
+The card on the board AND the inspector panel both register a `<FocusZone>` with the moniker `field:task:T1.title`. The kernel's `find_by_moniker` arbitrarily picks the first match in iteration order — apparently the BOARD's key — so `spatial_focus_by_moniker` advances focus into the BOARD layer's title-field zone. Then ArrowDown cascades from there → next card in the column → user perceives "nav spilled out of the inspector".
+
+This is independent of `useFirstFieldFocus` / claim-on-mount: the user reports the same bug after manually clicking the inspector field. The click handler dispatches `spatial_focus(zoneKey)` directly with the SpatialKey, but **whichever key the click handler resolved to depends on which DOM node receives the click**, and overlapping zones (or pointer-event leaks across the SlidePanel chrome) can route clicks to the wrong key. Even if the click resolved correctly, every other code path that uses `setFocus(moniker)` is still vulnerable to the duplicate-moniker race.
+
+## The architectural fix
+
+**Effective moniker = path.**
+
+- `FocusLayer.name` (e.g. `window`, `inspector`, `palette`) → contributes a path segment.
+- `FocusZone.moniker` (e.g. `card:T1`, `field:T1.title`) → contributes a path segment.
+- `FocusScope.moniker` (e.g. `tag:T1.bug`, `pill:assignee:alice`) → contributes a path segment.
+
+A primitive's effective moniker is computed at register time as `parent_path + "/" + local_segment`. The Rust kernel stores this path-moniker as the canonical identity. Lookup-by-moniker takes the path-moniker (or a partial path that matches a unique tail).
+
+The flat strings consumers pass today stay valid as the **local segment**. Only the kernel-side identity changes.
+
+### What `path` looks like
+
+```
+/window
+/window/board
+/window/board/column:todo
+/window/board/column:todo/card:T1
+/window/board/column:todo/card:T1/field:title          ← board's card-title
+/window/inspector
+/window/inspector/field:T1.title                        ← inspector's title (DIFFERENT!)
+/window/inspector/field:T1.tags
+/window/inspector/field:T1.tags/pill:tag:bug
+```
+
+Two registrations of "the same field component" in different render contexts produce different paths — the kernel disambiguates by context. No more duplicates.
+
+### What stays unchanged
+
+- The cascade rules (same-layer, same-kind, beam-search, drill-out) operate on path-monikers without any logic change. The path is just the new identity string.
+- `spatial_focus(SpatialKey)` is unchanged (key-based, already unambiguous).
+- The user-visible click-to-focus contract is unchanged.
+- Test fixtures that don't render duplicate hierarchies see identical monikers (`/window/board/...` is the only path — no other window's `/board/...` to collide with).
+
+## Approach — TDD
+
+Strict TDD against actual production behavior. Tests at three layers:
+
+### Layer 1 — Rust kernel tests (cargo)
+
+`swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/path_monikers.rs` (new file).
+
+- [ ] `register_zone_assigns_path_moniker_from_layer_and_zone_ancestors` — register a layer "inspector", a zone "panel:T1" inside it, a zone "field:T1.title" inside that. Assert the kernel stores their effective monikers as `/inspector`, `/inspector/panel:T1`, `/inspector/panel:T1/field:T1.title`.
+- [ ] `register_two_zones_with_same_local_segment_in_different_layers_produces_distinct_paths` — register `field:T1.title` under both `/window/board/card:T1` and `/window/inspector`. Assert no duplicate-moniker warning fires; both paths are findable.
+- [ ] `find_by_moniker_with_full_path_resolves_unambiguously` — given the two registrations above, `find_by_moniker("/window/inspector/field:T1.title")` returns the inspector's key, NOT the board's.
+- [ ] `find_by_moniker_with_local_segment_only_emits_warning_or_returns_topmost` — backward-compat behavior for callers that pass a flat string. Decide: (a) warn and return the topmost-layer match, OR (b) error. Pick (a) so existing callers degrade gracefully; flag the call site for migration.
+- [ ] `cascade_filtered_by_layer_does_not_consider_other_layer_paths` — register the duplicate fields above, focus the inspector's, dispatch `next(... Direction::Down)`, assert no candidate is from `/window/board/...`.
+
+### Layer 2 — React kernel-driven tests (vitest browser)
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/path-monikers.kernel-driven.browser.test.tsx` (new file).
+
+- [ ] `inspector_field_zone_registers_with_inspector_layer_path` — mount a real production tree (board + open inspector for a task). Snapshot the kernel-simulator's recorded registrations. Assert the inspector's title field's effective moniker is `/window/inspector/field:T1.title` (or whatever the inspector layer's name resolves to).
+- [ ] `card_field_zone_registers_with_board_layer_path` — same scene. Assert the board card's title field's effective moniker is `/window/board/column:todo/card:T1/field:title` (or whatever the actual board hierarchy produces).
+- [ ] `no_duplicate_moniker_warning_fires_when_inspector_opens` — wire the simulator to log warnings; assert zero warnings of class "duplicate moniker" after opening an inspector.
+- [ ] `set_focus_by_moniker_with_partial_path_resolves_to_topmost_layer` — call `setFocus("field:T1.title")` (the legacy flat form); assert the resolution lands on the inspector's path (the most-recently-pushed layer wins). Document this as the deprecated entry point.
+
+### Layer 3 — manual log verification (the actual production check)
+
+After Layers 1 and 2 pass, run `npm run tauri dev` and:
+
+- [ ] Open an inspector on a task. Run `log show --last 1m --predicate 'subsystem == "com.swissarmyhammer.kanban"' --info --debug | grep duplicate`. Assert zero output.
+- [ ] Click a field in the inspector. Press ArrowDown. Run the same `log show` query. Assert zero duplicate-moniker warnings AND focus stayed inside `/window/inspector/...` (visible in subsequent `ui.setFocus` scope_chain log lines).
+- [ ] Press ArrowDown at the last field. Assert focus echoes (stays put) — no `card:*` or `column:*` moniker appears in the scope_chain.
+- [ ] Press Escape. Assert the inspector closes and focus returns to the originating card via `last_focused`.
+
+The Layer-3 verification is **mandatory** before the task moves to done. Tests-pass-but-production-broken has happened twice on this surface; the failure mode is the simulator not modeling the Tauri kernel's exact registration behavior. Manual log inspection is the source of truth.
+
+## Implementation outline
+
+### Step 1 — Rust kernel: introduce path-moniker
+
+`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/registry.rs`:
+
+- Define `PathMoniker` (or extend `Moniker`) — a slash-delimited string. The leaf `Moniker` is preserved as a property on the registered scope.
+- `register_zone(key, local_moniker, parent_zone, layer_key, rect, overrides)` — at register time, compute the effective `path_moniker` by walking ancestors:
+  - parent_zone known: `parent_zone.path_moniker + "/" + local_moniker`
+  - parent_zone null: walk to the zone's layer, prepend `/layer_name`
+- Same for `register_scope`.
+- `push_layer(key, name, parent_layer)` — compute `path` by appending `/name` to parent layer's path.
+- `find_by_moniker(input)` — accept either full path (`/window/inspector/field:T1.title`) or a leaf segment. Full-path lookup is exact-match. Leaf lookup walks the registry; if multiple matches, prefer the one in the topmost (most-recently-pushed) layer; emit `tracing::warn!` flagging the call site for migration.
+
+### Step 2 — Tauri commands
+
+`kanban-app/src/commands.rs`:
+
+- `spatial_register_zone` — accept the local moniker; the kernel computes the path. The IPC payload doesn't change shape (callers still send `moniker: "field:T1.title"`); the kernel-side path is internal.
+- `spatial_focus_by_moniker` — accept either full-path or leaf form. Same disambiguation rule as `find_by_moniker`.
+
+### Step 3 — React adapter
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-layer.tsx`, `focus-zone.tsx`, `focus-scope.tsx`:
+
+- No change to the public API surface; consumers keep passing `moniker={...}`.
+- The kernel computes paths server-side; the React side needs no path awareness for register calls.
+- For `focus-changed` events, the kernel can emit BOTH the path and the leaf moniker. The entity-focus store keeps using the leaf moniker (so existing consumers' `useFocusedScope()` continues to return the leaf form).
+- For `setFocus(moniker)` calls that pass a leaf, the kernel's "topmost-layer wins" rule preserves the user's likely intent.
+
+### Step 4 — Migration + deprecation
+
+After step 1–3 land:
+
+- [ ] Audit every `setFocus(...)` and `actions.focus(...)` call. For places where the intent is "focus this in MY layer," upgrade to a path-aware call (or pass the SpatialKey when available).
+- [ ] The kernel's leaf-form lookup logs warnings for every legacy call so we can chase them.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] No `duplicate moniker registered against two distinct keys` warning appears in the running app's log when the inspector opens.
+- [ ] Clicking a field in an inspector advances spatial focus to the inspector's field zone — verified by `log show` showing the subsequent `ui.setFocus scope_chain` containing the inspector path, not the board path.
+- [ ] ArrowDown / ArrowUp inside the inspector stays inside the inspector layer — no `card:*` / `column:*` monikers appear in `ui.setFocus` scope_chain log lines.
+- [ ] ArrowDown at the last field echoes (focus stays put), no leak.
+- [ ] Escape closes the inspector and restores focus to the originating card via `last_focused` (existing layer-pop chain).
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` passes (incl. new path-moniker tests).
+- [ ] `bun run test:browser` passes (incl. new kernel-driven tests).
+- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.
+- [ ] All existing tests still pass — the change is internal to the kernel; the React API surface doesn't change.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- **Strict TDD with manual log verification.** Layers 1 and 2 first. Layer 3 (manual log inspection in the running Tauri app) is mandatory before declaring done — the past two passes on this surface had passing tests and broken production. The simulator does not model the duplicate-moniker bug because the simulator only sees one registration per moniker. The real Rust kernel is the source of truth for whether the bug is fixed.
+- Cross-reference: `01KQD0WK54G0FRD7SZVZASA9ST` (kernel-as-source-of-focus refactor — necessary precursor; this task supersedes its remaining edge cases). `01KQAW97R9XTCNR1PJAWYSKBC7` (no-silent-dropout contract — preserved).
+- Do not introduce claim-on-mount or per-rendering namespace hacks. Path-based monikers are the architectural answer.

Even if the click resolved correctly, every other code path that uses `setFocus(moniker)` is still vulnerable to the duplicate-moniker race.

## The architectural fix

**Effective moniker = path.**

- `FocusLayer.name` (e.g. `window`, `inspector`, `palette`) → contributes a path segment.
- `FocusZone.moniker` (e.g. `card:T1`, `field:T1.title`) → contributes a path segment.
- `FocusScope.moniker` (e.g. `tag:T1.bug`, `pill:assignee:alice`) → contributes a path segment.

A primitive's effective moniker is computed at register time as `parent_path + "/" + local_segment`. The Rust kernel stores this path-moniker as the canonical identity. Lookup-by-moniker takes the path-moniker (or a partial path that matches a unique tail).

The flat strings consumers pass today stay valid as the **local segment**. Only the kernel-side identity changes.

### What `path` looks like

```
/window
/window/board
/window/board/column:todo
/window/board/column:todo/card:T1
/window/board/column:todo/card:T1/field:title          ← board's card-title
/window/inspector
/window/inspector/field:T1.title                        ← inspector's title (DIFFERENT!)
/window/inspector/field:T1.tags
/window/inspector/field:T1.tags/pill:tag:bug
```

Two registrations of "the same field component" in different render contexts produce different paths — the kernel disambiguates by context. No more duplicates.

### What stays unchanged

- The cascade rules (same-layer, same-kind, beam-search, drill-out) operate on path-monikers without any logic change. The path is just the new identity string.
- `spatial_focus(SpatialKey)` is unchanged (key-based, already unambiguous).
- The user-visible click-to-focus contract is unchanged.
- Test fixtures that don't render duplicate hierarchies see identical monikers (`/window/board/...` is the only path — no other window's `/board/...` to collide with).

## Approach — TDD

Strict TDD against actual production behavior. Tests at three layers:

### Layer 1 — Rust kernel tests (cargo)

`swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/path_monikers.rs` (new file).

- [ ] `register_zone_assigns_path_moniker_from_layer_and_zone_ancestors` — register a layer "inspector", a zone "panel:T1" inside it, a zone "field:T1.title" inside that. Assert the kernel stores their effective monikers as `/inspector`, `/inspector/panel:T1`, `/inspector/panel:T1/field:T1.title`.
- [ ] `register_two_zones_with_same_local_segment_in_different_layers_produces_distinct_paths` — register `field:T1.title` under both `/window/board/card:T1` and `/window/inspector`. Assert no duplicate-moniker warning fires; both paths are findable.
- [ ] `find_by_moniker_with_full_path_resolves_unambiguously` — given the two registrations above, `find_by_moniker("/window/inspector/field:T1.title")` returns the inspector's key, NOT the board's.
- [ ] `find_by_moniker_with_local_segment_only_emits_warning_or_returns_topmost` — backward-compat behavior for callers that pass a flat string. Decide: (a) warn and return the topmost-layer match, OR (b) error. Pick (a) so existing callers degrade gracefully; flag the call site for migration.
- [ ] `cascade_filtered_by_layer_does_not_consider_other_layer_paths` — register the duplicate fields above, focus the inspector's, dispatch `next(... Direction::Down)`, assert no candidate is from `/window/board/...`.

### Layer 2 — React kernel-driven tests (vitest browser)

`kanban-app/ui/src/components/path-monikers.kernel-driven.browser.test.tsx` (new file).

- [ ] `inspector_field_zone_registers_with_inspector_layer_path` — mount a real production tree (board + open inspector for a task). Snapshot the kernel-simulator's recorded registrations. Assert the inspector's title field's effective moniker is `/window/inspector/field:T1.title` (or whatever the inspector layer's name resolves to).
- [ ] `card_field_zone_registers_with_board_layer_path` — same scene. Assert the board card's title field's effective moniker is `/window/board/column:todo/card:T1/field:title` (or whatever the actual board hierarchy produces).
- [ ] `no_duplicate_moniker_warning_fires_when_inspector_opens` — wire the simulator to log warnings; assert zero warnings of class "duplicate moniker" after opening an inspector.
- [ ] `set_focus_by_moniker_with_partial_path_resolves_to_topmost_layer` — call `setFocus("field:T1.title")` (the legacy flat form); assert the resolution lands on the inspector's path (the most-recently-pushed layer wins). Document this as the deprecated entry point.

### Layer 3 — manual log verification (the actual production check)

After Layers 1 and 2 pass, run `npm run tauri dev` and:

- [ ] Open an inspector on a task. Run `log show --last 1m --predicate 'subsystem == "com.swissarmyhammer.kanban"' --info --debug | grep duplicate`. Assert zero output.
- [ ] Click a field in the inspector. Press ArrowDown. Run the same `log show` query. Assert zero duplicate-moniker warnings AND focus stayed inside `/window/inspector/...` (visible in subsequent `ui.setFocus` scope_chain log lines).
- [ ] Press ArrowDown at the last field. Assert focus echoes (stays put) — no `card:*` or `column:*` moniker appears in the scope_chain.
- [ ] Press Escape. Assert the inspector closes and focus returns to the originating card via `last_focused`.

The Layer-3 verification is **mandatory** before the task moves to done. Tests-pass-but-production-broken has happened twice on this surface; the failure mode is the simulator not modeling the Tauri kernel's exact registration behavior. Manual log inspection is the source of truth.

## Implementation outline

### Step 1 — Rust kernel: introduce path-moniker

`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/registry.rs`:

- Define `PathMoniker` (or extend `Moniker`) — a slash-delimited string. The leaf `Moniker` is preserved as a property on the registered scope.
- `register_zone(key, local_moniker, parent_zone, layer_key, rect, overrides)` — at register time, compute the effective `path_moniker` by walking ancestors:
  - parent_zone known: `parent_zone.path_moniker + "/" + local_moniker`
  - parent_zone null: walk to the zone's layer, prepend `/layer_name`
- Same for `register_scope`.
- `push_layer(key, name, parent_layer)` — compute `path` by appending `/name` to parent layer's path.
- `find_by_moniker(input)` — accept either full path (`/window/inspector/field:T1.title`) or a leaf segment. Full-path lookup is exact-match. Leaf lookup walks the registry; if multiple matches, prefer the one in the topmost (most-recently-pushed) layer; emit `tracing::warn!` flagging the call site for migration.

### Step 2 — Tauri commands

`kanban-app/src/commands.rs`:

- `spatial_register_zone` — accept the local moniker; the kernel computes the path. The IPC payload doesn't change shape (callers still send `moniker: "field:T1.title"`); the kernel-side path is internal.
- `spatial_focus_by_moniker` — accept either full-path or leaf form. Same disambiguation rule as `find_by_moniker`.

### Step 3 — React adapter

`kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-layer.tsx`, `focus-zone.tsx`, `focus-scope.tsx`:

- No change to the public API surface; consumers keep passing `moniker={...}`.
- The kernel computes paths server-side; the React side needs no path awareness for register calls.
- For `focus-changed` events, the kernel can emit BOTH the path and the leaf moniker. The entity-focus store keeps using the leaf moniker (so existing consumers' `useFocusedScope()` continues to return the leaf form).
- For `setFocus(moniker)` calls that pass a leaf, the kernel's "topmost-layer wins" rule preserves the user's likely intent.

### Step 4 — Migration + deprecation

After step 1–3 land:

- [ ] Audit every `setFocus(...)` and `actions.focus(...)` call. For places where the intent is "focus this in MY layer," upgrade to a path-aware call (or pass the SpatialKey when available).
- [ ] The kernel's leaf-form lookup logs warnings for every legacy call so we can chase them.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No `duplicate moniker registered against two distinct keys` warning appears in the running app's log when the inspector opens.
- [ ] Clicking a field in an inspector advances spatial focus to the inspector's field zone — verified by `log show` showing the subsequent `ui.setFocus scope_chain` containing the inspector path, not the board path.
- [ ] ArrowDown / ArrowUp inside the inspector stays inside the inspector layer — no `card:*` / `column:*` monikers appear in `ui.setFocus` scope_chain log lines.
- [ ] ArrowDown at the last field echoes (focus stays put), no leak.
- [ ] Escape closes the inspector and restores focus to the originating card via `last_focused` (existing layer-pop chain).
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` passes (incl. new path-moniker tests).
- [ ] `bun run test:browser` passes (incl. new kernel-driven tests).
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.
- [ ] All existing tests still pass — the change is internal to the kernel; the React API surface doesn't change.

## Workflow

- **Strict TDD with manual log verification.** Layers 1 and 2 first. Layer 3 (manual log inspection in the running Tauri app) is mandatory before declaring done — the past two passes on this surface had passing tests and broken production. The simulator does not model the duplicate-moniker bug because the simulator only sees one registration per moniker. The real Rust kernel is the source of truth for whether the bug is fixed.

- Do not introduce claim-on-mount or per-rendering namespace hacks. Path-based monikers are the architectural answer.
